name: ESP8266 Platform
description: This component contains platform-specific options for the ESP8266 platform.

config_entries.label: Board
config_entries.description: The PlatformIO board ID that should be used. Choose the appropriate board from [this list](https://registry.platformio.org/platforms/platformio/espressif8266/boards) (the icon next to the name can be used to copy the board ID). *This only affects pin aliases, flash size and some internal settings*, if unsure choose a generic board from Espressif such as `esp01_1m`.

config_entries.label: Framework
config_entries.description: Options for the underlying framework used by ESPHome.

config_entries.label: Platform Version
config_entries.description: The version of the [platformio/espressif8266](https://github.com/platformio/platform-espressif8266/releases/) package to use.

config_entries.label: Source
config_entries.description: The PlatformIO package or repository to use for the framework. This can be used to use a custom or patched version of the framework.

config_entries.label: Version
config_entries.description: The base framework version number to use, from [esp8266 arduino releases](https://github.com/esp8266/Arduino/releases). Defaults to `recommended`. Additional values

config_entries.label: Board Flash Mode
config_entries.description: The SPI mode of the flash chip. One of `qio`, `qout`, `dio` and `dout`. Defaults to `dout` for compatibility with all chips. Note: on the next OTA update the actual flash mode is automatically detected and changed to the appropriate one.

config_entries.label: Early Pin Init
config_entries.description: Specifies whether pins should be initialised as early as possible to known values. Recommended value is `false` where switches are involved, as these will toggle when updating the firmware or when restarting the device. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Enable Full Printf
config_entries.description: Enable full `FILE*`-based printf support. By default, ESPHome wraps `printf()`, `vprintf()`, and `fprintf()` with lightweight stubs that use `vsnprintf()` + `fwrite()`, saving ~1.6 KB of flash. ESPHome logging writes directly to the UART via Arduino's `Serial`, not libc printf, so these functions are typically unused unless an external component calls them. Set to `true` only if an external component needs full `FILE*`-based printf. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Enable Scanf Float
config_entries.description: Enable float support for `scanf()`/`sscanf()`. By default, ESPHome removes the `-u _scanf_float` linker flag to save ~8 KB of flash. This means `sscanf()` with `%f` will silently fail to parse floating-point numbers. Nothing in ESPHome itself uses scanf; set to `true` only if you use `sscanf()` with `%f` in lambdas. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Enable Serial
config_entries.description: Force-enable the Arduino `Serial` object (UART0) for use in lambdas or external libraries. Most users will never need this option, as the `logger` and `uart` components automatically enable the required Serial objects. Only use this if you directly access `Serial` in a lambda and get a compilation error. Use the [UART component](https://esphome.io/components/uart) instead when possible, as it works across all platforms. Defaults to automatic detection.

config_entries.label: Enable Serial1
config_entries.description: Force-enable the Arduino `Serial1` object (UART1) for use in lambdas or external libraries. Most users will never need this option, as the `logger` and `uart` components automatically enable the required Serial objects. Only use this if you directly access `Serial1` in a lambda and get a compilation error. Use the [UART component](https://esphome.io/components/uart) instead when possible, as it works across all platforms. Defaults to automatic detection.

config_entries.label: Restore From Flash
config_entries.description: Whether to store some persistent preferences in flash memory. Defaults to `false`.
